title: Java Interface Methods
description: Practice default, static, and private interface methods.
learningGoals: Recall which interface methods are inherited and which are called through the interface type.
Recognize that private interface helpers are only callable inside the interface.

choices: Compilation fails because tax cannot call the private clamp
completionChecks.explanation: fixed(0.2) returns a Taxable whose rate() is 0.2, and the default tax(100) computes clamp(100) * rate() = 100 * 0.2 = 20.0; a default method may call a private interface helper.

completionChecks.prompt: How is a static interface method called?
choices: Through super from an unrelated class
Through an instance reference such as new X().fixed(...)
Through the interface name, such as Taxable.fixed(...)
completionChecks.explanation: static interface methods are not inherited by implementing classes or instances; you call them through the interface name, such as Taxable.fixed(0.2).
The default method calls clamp first, and Math.max(0.0, -50) produces 0.0 before multiplying by the fixed rate.
